name: SOLIGENIX, INC.
business: Soligenix is a late-stage biopharmaceutical company developing therapies for rare diseases, with a focus on HyBryte for cutaneous T-cell lymphoma (CTCL). It maintains two active business segments: Specialized BioTherapeutics and Public Health Solutions.
sellsTo: Patients with rare diseases (CTCL, psoriasis, Behçet's disease, oral mucositis)
U.S. government agencies for biodefense products

exposures.sentence: We are a late-stage biopharmaceutical company focused on developing and commercializing products to treat rare diseases where there is an unmet medical need.

Our Public Health Solutions business segment includes development programs for (i) RiVax, a ricin toxin vaccine candidate, (ii) SGX943, a therapeutic candidate for antibiotic resistant and emerging infectious disease, and (iii) various vaccine programs, including a program targeting filoviruses (such as Marburg virus and Ebola virus) and CiVax, a vaccine candidate for the prevention of COVID-19.

catalysts.event: Interim analysis for FLASH2 Phase 3 trial
catalysts.sentence: With successful completion of the first Phase 3 FLASH study and agreement from the European Medicines Agency on the key design components of a confirmatory Phase 3 placebo-controlled study evaluating the safety and efficacy of HyBryte in the treatment of CTCL patients with early-stage disease, we began patient enrollment during December 2024 for the second Phase 3 study called FLASH2 (Fluorescent Light Activated Synthetic Hypericin 2). We anticipate top-line results in the second half of 2026.

catalysts.event: Top-line results for FLASH2 Phase 3 trial
catalysts.sentence: We anticipate top-line results in the second half of 2026.

tamClaims.claim: Potential worldwide market for HyBryte for CTCL exceeds $250 million
tamClaims.sentence: We estimate the potential worldwide market for HyBryte is in excess of $250 million for the treatment of CTCL.

tamClaims.claim: Potential worldwide market for SGX302 for psoriasis exceeds $1 billion
tamClaims.sentence: We estimate the potential worldwide market for SGX302 is in excess of $1 billion for the treatment of mild-to-moderate psoriasis.

tamClaims.claim: Potential worldwide market for SGX945 for Behçet's disease exceeds $200 million
tamClaims.sentence: We estimate the potential worldwide market for SGX945 is in excess of $200 million for the treatment of aphthous ulcers in BD.
